macro_segments.title: Introduction and Review of Erusin, Divorce, and Re-betrothal Framework

micro_segments.title: Opening and Daf Overview
Mishnah: Father and Last Groom Annul Vows Despite Multiple Divorces and Re-betrothals
Principle: Father as Anchor — Vows Remain in Father's Control Until Daughter's Independence

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Challenge: Does the Last Groom Annul Vows Heard by the First Groom?

micro_segments.title: Question: Can Last Groom Annul Vows Previously Heard by First Groom (Niru L'Arus Rishon)?
Shmuel's Proof from Verse: Double Language (Hayo/Tihyeh) Implies Vows Transfer Between Grooms
Gemara: Even if First Groom Heard, Can Second Groom Annul? Shmuel Asserts Yes.

macro_segments.title: Braita Proving Shmuel's Point: Groom Heard, Died, Father and Next Groom Can Still Annul

micro_segments.title: Braita Opening: Betrothed Maiden, Father and Husband Annul Vows
Scenario 1: Father Hears and Annuls, First Groom Dies Before Hearing
Scenario 2: Groom Hears and Annuls, Dies Before Father Hears — Father Annuls His Portion
Beit Shammai vs. Beit Hillel: Transfer of Groom's Right After He Performs Hafara and Dies

macro_segments.title: Interpretation of Beit Shammai and Beit Hillel Dispute: Role-Staking and Transferability

micro_segments.title: Beit Shammai: Groom's Right Transfers Back to Father (Migaz Gayiz) Even After He Performed Hafara
Beit Hillel: Once Groom Stakes His Role, No Transfer Possible — Vow Dies with Him
Gemara's Reading: Debate Hinges on Whether Groom Performed Hafara

macro_segments.title: New Question: Does Divorce (Gerushin) Constitute Hakama (Affirmation of Vow)?

micro_segments.title: Question Posed: Is Gerushin Like Shtika (Silence) or Like Hakama (Affirmation)?
Implications of Gerushin as Hakama: If Groom Divorces, Can Father Still Annul?
Practical Case: Groom Hears Vow, Divorces, Then Remarries Same Day

macro_segments.title: First Proof Attempt: Can Last Groom Annul If Vow Was Affirmed by First Groom?

micro_segments.title: If Gerushin Were Hakama, Could Last Groom Annul a Vow the First Affirmed?
Gemara: Our Mishnah Assumes First Groom Did Not Hear Vow

macro_segments.title: Testing Gerushin as Hakama: Explicit Braita on Divorce and Remarriage Same Day

micro_segments.title: Braita: Vow Made, Divorced, Remarried Same Day — Groom Cannot Annul
Proof Extracted: Gerushin Appears to Function as Hakama
Counter: Case Is of Married Woman (Nesuah), Not Betrothed — Different Rule (Lo Mefer B'Kodmin)
Ran's Analysis: Why Both Marriages Cannot Solve the Nesuah Problem

macro_segments.title: Unresolved Question: Nature of Hakama and Requirements for Its Performance

micro_segments.title: Summary: Gerushin Status (Hakama vs. Shtika) Remains Unproven from Available Sources
Broader Issue: Does Hakama Require Verbal Act or Can It Be Demonstrated by Conduct?

topical_tags.term: Niru L'Arus Rishon (heard by first groom)
